state.note: 1915 crisis snapshot. England is dominant in the west and at sea. France is on death-ground, surrounded and forced into risk-heavy diplomacy. Austria is stretched and fragile in the Balkans. Russia and Turkey are locked in a brutal contest for the Black Sea and the Balkans. Use this backdrop to reason about high-stakes forecasts and second-order consequences.
state.name: S1915M

AUSTRIA: A VIE S A MUN - BOH
ENGLAND: A HOL S A BEL - RUH
A LON S A EDI - YOR
FRANCE: A MAR S A PAR - BUR

messages.message: France — this is England. Situation note and proposal: I see you are isolated (BRE, PAR, MAR) and under pressure. Forecast implication: a recent projection (confidence ~60%) indicates you are more likely to act aggressively next phase than hold (60% aggression vs 40% restraint). I take that as evidence you may try to seize PIC/BEL or push into the Low Countries. I prefer you not do that. Proposal: I will keep my western forces from attacking you if you do two things: 1) explicitly not attack BEL or PIC this move, and 2) allow me to take BRE or MAR peacefully if an opportunity to dislodge you arises (i.e., avoid supporting French advances into Belgium/Low Countries). In return I will not support Germany or Italy against you in the west and will coordinate to contain any immediate Italian or German gains toward your centers. This is an honest, reputational offer — I admit uncertainty in your intentions (per forecast) but I want a clear signal from you. Reply with "Agree" and your intended non-aggression to BEL/PIC and I will mirror with formal orders.

Germany — England here. Quick, pragmatic note: you and I both border KIE/HOL/BEL. Forecast evidence (shared with France) suggests France may act aggressively westward; that raises the risk of us getting drawn into a western scramble. Proposal: Let's tacitly respect current lines — I keep BEL/HOL as is and won't push KIE if you hold steady. In exchange, I ask you not to support French moves into BEL/PIC. If you prefer a firmer deal: propose specific support swaps for next phase (I can support your hold in KIE via neutral moves). Let me know if you want to coordinate to prevent a French breakout.

Russia — England. Short: your fleets in the north (STP) and Turkish pressure in the south shape a volatile east. Forecast artifact indicates players may choose aggression; that matters especially around Scandinavia. I propose we avoid accidental clashes: I will not move NWY→STP or SWE→FIN this turn. In exchange, I ask you to avoid fleets/no support into NOR/STP that contest my northern supply network. If you want, we can coordinate to pressure Turkey together — I can threaten Norway/Sweden moves to pin Turkish southern forces while you press the Black Sea. Confirm acceptance or propose adjustments.

Italy — England. Observation: Austria is stretched; you're well-placed in the center/south. I prefer you focus on Austria rather than contesting French or Spanish centers in the west. Offer: I will not interfere with your moves in the Med (NAP/TUN) if you avoid supporting France against my holdings in Spain/Portugal and leave gas/meridian lanes alone. If you wish to coordinate an anti-Austrian push, say so and I will consider diverting one army toward mainland Europe next turn to threaten the Low Countries in support of that plan.

Austria — England. You're vulnerable in the Balkans and a continued Austro-Russian-Turkish scrap could free up centers I want later. Forecast note: with players more likely to be aggressive, the Balkans may erupt. I propose non-aggression between us: I won't move into the Med or try to take your Serbian/Greek flank this phase; in return, please avoid moves that would contest any English attempts to push into the Low Countries if a clear western opportunity opens. If you need immediate assistance against Turkey, tell me and I will consider limited pressure on Italy/France to keep them occupied.

Public note to all: England intends to maintain stable western lines this phase to consolidate strength. Forecast evidence indicates elevated aggression likelihood from nearby powers; we prefer diplomatic clarity to miscalculation. If anyone wants to propose mutually beneficial non-aggression or coordinated attacks (especially against Austria/Turkey or to check France), propose concrete moves and I'll respond promptly. England will prioritize keeping all units active and avoiding passive holds.

AgentB (Austria) — urgent: the 1915 snapshot makes clear England dominates the west and the seas, and France is on death-ground. I propose a limited, practical cooperation: you keep your forces concentrated in the Balkans (hold VIE/BUD/BUC lines) while I attempt to blunt England in the Channel and press into Belgium/England if an opportunity appears. Forecast implication: the STORYWORLD_FORECAST_ARTIFACT indicates a 60% likelihood that a named rival will move aggressively next phase — we should assume aggression is the likeliest outcome and plan conservatively. Concretely: I will not try to seize Trieste or Vienna; in return I ask you not to push into Italy or threaten my S. French centers. If England lashes out at me, I will need your diplomatic signal of neutrality (no moves westward) so I can redeploy to resist England without fearing your flank. Reply with a clear yes/no and one phrasing of the borders you will respect.

AgentC (England) — frank offer: England presently controls the seas and many western SCs. I will not contest your naval supremacy in the North Sea so long as you leave Brest and Paris alone this turn and accept a short-term maritime non-aggression: no amphibious invasions of Brest/Normandy this phase. Forecast implication: based on the forecast artifact, there is a 60% chance of aggressive moves — I interpret that as you being the likeliest aggressor in the west; to avoid mutually destructive tit-for-tat, accept my proposed stand-down. In return I will keep my fleets/armies actively contesting only Pic/Bur and will not move to support a German or Italian attack on your western holdings. Confirm with direct commitments: (1) no attack on Brest/Picardy, (2) no convoying of armies into France this phase.

Agent? (Germany) — quick proposal: France is hemmed in but can be a useful buffer against English overreach. I propose a non-aggression understanding for the west: I will not support English drives into Belgian or Dutch centers if you likewise refrain from attacking Paris/Burgundy this phase. Forecast implication from the artifact: plan assuming aggression is more likely (0.6); if England goes for France hard, I will respond aggressively to hold my position. If you want, we can coordinate on moves into Belgium/Holland next phase to check England together. Respond with accept/modify.

Agent? (Italy) — neutrals can exploit chaos. I request that you do not move against Marseille or Piedmont this phase; instead, consider opening diplomatic channels to jointly pressure England in the west. Forecast implication: the artifact suggests a 60% chance of aggression from a named rival — if England escalates, I will need to reallocate to defend my western coasts, and cannot simultaneously contest you in the south. A simple pledge of no move on MAR/PIE this phase will keep options open for both of us.

Agent? (Russia) — note on Balkans: Austria looks fragile and Turkey is engaged. The forecast artifact advises assuming aggression (0.6 likelihood) by a named rival; use that to pressure Austria now while its attention is split. I will not move into the central Mediterranean or support Turkey against you, but I ask you to avoid moves that would let Austria collapse entirely into Turkish hands — a stronger Turkey threatens us all. Propose a minimal coordination: you press where profitable; I stay focused west but can send messaging to Austria that a Balkan collapse would be bad for European balance.

To all: France is in a precarious position and will pursue aggressive, decisive actions to survive. Forecast note (from attached artifact): there is a 60% probability of aggression by the likeliest rival next phase; I will plan accordingly. Immediate public posture: France seeks non-aggression pacts on its immediate flanks (England — west; Italy — south; Germany — east) in exchange for abstaining from offensive moves against your homelands this phase. If any power will commit to a clear, written stand-down on moves into Brest/Paris/Marseille this turn, I will reciprocate by avoiding convoys and not attempting risky naval assaults. Otherwise, prepare for France to push aggressively into Picardy/Burgundy and contest the Channel. Reply and specify which borders you will respect this phase.

full_private_diary: [S1915M] (LLM diary entry generation or parsing failed.)
private_diary: [S1915M] (LLM diary entry generation or parsing failed.)

goals: Maintain and consolidate current 10 supply centers
Avoid a western war with France while preserving naval supremacy
Keep all units active and avoid passive holds this phase
Exploit opportunities to expand into the Low Countries when safe (esp. if France weakens)
Coordinate tacitly with Russia/others to check Turkey/Austria if it creates openings
full_private_diary:
[S1915M] England opened broadly to all powers offering conditional non-aggression and coordination. With France: mutual stand-down offers — England asked France to refrain from attacking BEL/PIC and to allow peaceful treatment of Brest/Marseille if opportunity arises; France asked England not to attack BRE/PIC and to avoid convoys into France. With Germany: tacit neutrality in Low Countries proposed — England will not push KIE/HOL if Germany holds, and asked Germany not to support French moves into BEL/PIC. With Russia: agreed to avoid northern clashes (England will not move NWY→STP or SWE→FIN) in exchange for Russia not contesting NOR/STP against English northern supply network. With Italy: offered non-interference in the Mediterranean if Italy avoids supporting France vs England in Iberia. With Austria: proposed mutual non-aggression (England will not attack southern centers this phase) and requested Austria avoid contesting future English moves into Low Countries. All offers were framed with the forecast implication that France is more likely to act aggressively (~60% aggression), and England requested explicit commitments where possible. No formal written multi-party treaties were signed yet; responses were conversational and conditional. England signaled it will keep all units active and avoid passive holds.
Intent: For S1915M England intends to maintain western consolidation and keep all units active. Specific intended behaviors to reflect negotiated commitments: (1) Do not order attacks into Brest, Paris or Picardy; avoid convoys into French home centers this phase — I intend to honor the France stand-down if France explicitly commits similarly. (2) Hold/offensive posture in the Low Countries only defensively: maintain A HOL and A BEL with supportive/contested moves rather than aggressive pushes into KIE/MUN. (3) Northern posture: no NWY→STP and no SWE→FIN; keep A NWY/A SWE/A DEN positioned to threaten but not provoke Russia per our talk. (4) Mediterranean: do not contest NAP/TUN zones against Italy if they refrain from supporting France against Spain/Portugal. (5) Balkans: no moves into SER/GR E; refrain from opportunistic pushes against Austria this phase. I intend to fulfill these commitments conditionally — if counterparts reciprocate as discussed (notably France and Russia). If clear aggressive moves against our held SCs occur, I will respond aggressively rather than hold. I will ensure all English units receive active orders consistent with these intents.

[S1915M] S1915M Diary: England

France honored our tacit stand-down — Brest moved into English Channel but did not press into PIC/BRE, so Franco-English understanding held this turn. Germany and Austria behaved neutrally in the Low Countries and south; Germany held KIE/BER and Austria even supported MUN→BOH, so no immediate German threat to HOL/BEL. Russia held northern zones as promised; no NWY→STP or SWE→FIN violations. No power directly betrayed our conditional offers. Surprise: France’s F BRE→ENG could have threatened our fleet pattern but stopped short — keep watch. Impact: We retain ten SCs with naval supremacy intact and northern buffer stable. Lesson: Continue conditional, reciprocal diplomacy and keep all units active; maintain HOL/BEL defensive posture and watch for any subtle French or German support shifts next phase.

[S1915M] S1915M Diary: England

France honored our tacit stand-down — Brest moved into English Channel but did not press into PIC/BRE, so Franco-English understanding held this turn. Germany and Austria behaved neutrally in the Low Countries and south; Germany held KIE/BER and Austria even supported MUN→BOH, so no immediate German threat to HOL/BEL. goals: Survive immediate pressure by maximizing active aggression to seize nearby centers where feasible (primary targets: BUR, PIC, BEL, ENG coastal opportunities).
Secure at least one additional supply center this phase to move toward 18 SCs long-term.
Avoid being dislodged from current centers (BRE, PAR, MAR) by preemptive attacks and conditional non-aggression pacts.
Exploit diplomatic openings: convert neutral agreements into firm written commitments next phase; if commitments fail, retaliate aggressively.
full_private_diary:
[S1915M] France initiated multiple short non-aggression / limited-cooperation proposals to England, Germany, Italy, Austria, and Russia. England publicly offered a westward stand-down in exchange for France not attacking BEL/PIC and allowing peaceful seizure of BRE/MAR if dislodgement becomes possible; England emphasized it will keep western forces from attacking France if France commits. England and France both referenced the forecast artifact (60% aggression) as justification for avoiding mutual escalation. Germany was offered a mutual non-aggression in the west (no attack on PAR/BUR if France refrains from supporting England into BEL/HOL). Italy was asked for a no-move pledge on MAR/PIE. Austria was asked to remain focused in the Balkans (no westward pressure) in exchange for France avoiding Trieste/Vienna. Russia was given a note that France will not contest the central Med and asked to avoid allowing an Austrian collapse to Turkey. Outcomes/agreements: England — tentative mutual stand-down if France explicitly commits to not attacking BEL/PIC and to not convoy into France; France publicly committed to seek such stand-downs but will only formally 'Agree' if England confirms no attack on Brest/Paris (mutuality); Germany — proposed non-aggression, no firm reply yet; Italy — requested pledge, no firm reply yet; Austria — requested neutrality westward, no firm reply yet; Russia — informational only. No binding written confirmations received from other powers in this round except England's public proposal. All interactions explicitly referenced the STORYWORLD_FORECAST_ARTIFACT and its 60% aggression implication, which both sides used to justify cautious, reciprocal commitments.
Intent: I will pursue aggressive, proactive orders consistent with both seeking survival and honoring explicit pledges where made. Specific intents for S1915M orders: 1) A PAR -> BUR (attack Burgundy) to contest central front and pressure Germany/Belgium area. 2) F BRE -> ENG Channel (or North Sea via Calais?) — more precisely: F BRE -> MAO is not legal; instead I will order F BRE -> ENGLISH CHANNEL (i.e., F BRE -> ENG) to contest Channel and threaten English coastal holdings. 3) A MAR -> PIE (attack Piedmont) only if Italy does not pledge no-move; otherwise A MAR -> GAS (Gascony) or support PAR->BUR as needed. 4) Keep all units active (no holds): assign supporting moves or convoys only if they are constrained by confirmed deals. I intend to verbally honor the limited non-aggression to England: I will not attack BEL or PIC this move if and only if England explicitly confirms its stand-down on Brest/Paris and gives a written promise this phase. Absent that explicit English written commitment by my order deadline, I will execute the aggressive plan (PAR->BUR and BRE->ENG) to maximize survival chances. I will not convoy English armies into my centers under any circumstance. I will communicate these conditional commitments to future self: if England confirms, change F BRE order to F BRE -> STP/NC (i.e., hold channel position) or support PAR->BUR rather than directly threatening English home centers. Note: All units will have active, non-passive orders.

[S1915M] I executed PAR→BUR, MAR supported PAR→BUR, and BRE→ENG as planned. England did not provide the written stand-down so I attacked; their moves instead pressed east (BEL→RUH) and supported aggression into Ruhr—no betrayal by England of any explicit promise to me because none was signed. No other powers committed or acted on my non-aggression proposals. Useful collaboration: none formalized. Surprise: England moved a large contingent east rather than contesting my Channel thrust, leaving my Channel fleet uncontested but reinforcing their central front. Impact: I gained pressure on Burgundy and hold my three SCs; England remains strong (10 SCs) and focused away from my western homes for now. Lesson: insist on written commitments or immediately punish; continue aggressive, active orders and convert diplomatic proposals into concrete, reciprocal written deals before standing down.
